edges.description: Constitutively active BRAF continuously phosphorylates MEK and ERK, driving growth-promoting transcription independent of upstream growth-factor signaling.
edges.source: BRAF V600E and MAPK-Pathway Activating Mutation

edges.description: Constitutive SMO activation drives GLI-dependent transcription of proliferation and differentiation genes in odontogenic epithelium.
Expanding tumor epithelium infiltrates medullary spaces and resorbs cortical bone of the jaw.
Progressive tumor growth and cortical expansion produce painless jaw swelling.
Tumor expansion displaces teeth within the affected jaw region.
Unilateral jaw expansion produces visible facial deformity.
Tumor involvement of the inferior alveolar nerve produces altered sensation.

edges.target: BRAF V600E and MAPK-Pathway Activating Mutation

nodes.description: Somatic activating mutations in the MAPK pathway, most commonly BRAF V600E (valine-to-glutamic-acid substitution at codon 600; pooled prevalence ~70% across 833 cases), lock BRAF kinase in a constitutively active state. Additional MAPK drivers include FGFR2 (upstream RTK), KRAS, NRAS, and HRAS. These mutations are most frequent in mandibular tumors.
nodes.id: BRAF V600E and MAPK-Pathway Activating Mutation

evidence.reference_title: BRAF V600E Mutation in Ameloblastoma: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
evidence.snippet: Meta-analysis prevalence of BRAF V600E in ameloblastoma was 70.49%, and a significant meta-analysis association was reported for those younger than 54 years old and in the mandible.

evidence.reference_title: New Ameloblastoma Cell Lines Enable Preclinical Study of Targeted Therapies.
evidence.snippet: we report the establishment of 6 new AB cell lines-generated by "conditional reprogramming"-and their genomic characterization that reveals driver mutations in FGFR2, KRAS, NRAS, BRAF, PIK3CA, and SMO.

nodes.description: Pharmacotherapy targeting the MAPK pathway in BRAF V600E-mutant ameloblastoma, typically dabrafenib (BRAF inhibitor) combined with trametinib (MEK inhibitor), or vemurafenib. Used as neoadjuvant therapy to shrink tumors before conservative surgery, and in recurrent, metastatic, or unresectable disease. A systematic review of 23 patients reported that nearly all responded, with 4 complete radiologic remissions.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-MAPK Targeted Therapy for Ameloblastoma: Case Report with a Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: covering 23 patients treated with MAPK inhibitor therapies. The results were promising as nearly all patients showed a positive treatment response, with four achieving complete radiological remission and others showing substantial reductions in primary, recurrent, and metastatic ameloblastoma sizes.

evidence.reference_title: BRAF inhibitors in BRAF V600E-mutated ameloblastoma: systematic review of rare cases in the literature.
evidence.snippet: These included seven case reports with nine patients who underwent monotherapy with Dabrafenib or Vemurafenib or combination therapy with Dabrafenib and Trametinib.

nodes.description: Convergent MAPK and Hedgehog signaling drive uncontrolled proliferation of odontogenic epithelial cells with evasion of apoptosis, producing the islands, cords, and sheets of tumor epithelium characteristic of ameloblastoma.

evidence.reference_title: Identification of Molecular Mechanisms of Ameloblastoma and Drug Repositioning by Integration of Bioinformatics Analysis and Molecular Docking Simulation.
evidence.snippet: Ameloblastoma (AM) is a benign tumor locally originated from odontogenic epithelium that is commonly found in the jaw. This tumor makes aggressive invasions and has a high recurrence rate.

nodes.description: Noticeable facial deformity resulting from unilateral jaw expansion in established disease.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of BRAF Inhibitors in the Management of Ameloblastoma: A Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: This neoplasia is distinguished by exhibiting several clinical and histological variants along with several mutations that affect its behavior.

nodes.description: Activating mutations in SMO (Smoothened), most commonly SMO-L412F and less commonly SMO-W535L, drive ligand-independent Hedgehog signaling and GLI-driven transcription. SMO mutations are more typical of maxillary ameloblastomas and represent an alternative oncogenic route to odontogenic epithelial proliferation.

evidence.reference_title: New Ameloblastoma Cell Lines Enable Preclinical Study of Targeted Therapies.
evidence.snippet: AB cells with activating SMO-L412F mutation (Hedgehog pathway) are insensitive to vismodegib; however, a distinct small-molecule SMO inhibitor, BMS-833923, significantly reduces both downstream Hedgehog signaling and tumor cell viability.

nodes.description: Painless, slowly progressive swelling of the mandible or maxilla with expansion of buccal and lingual cortical plates; present in nearly all cases at diagnosis.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-MAPK Targeted Therapy for Ameloblastoma: Case Report with a Systematic Review.
evidence.snippet: Ameloblastoma, a benign yet aggressive odontogenic tumor known for its recurrence and the severe morbidity from radical surgeries, may benefit from advancements in targeted therapy.

nodes.description: The locally invasive tumor infiltrates through the medullary spaces of the jawbone and resorbs cortical bone, destroying normal bone architecture and creating the characteristic unilocular or multilocular radiolucencies. Infiltration beyond radiographic margins underlies the high recurrence rate.

evidence.reference_title: New Ameloblastoma Cell Lines Enable Preclinical Study of Targeted Therapies.
evidence.snippet: Although relatively uncommon and rarely metastatic, AB tumors are locally invasive and destructive to the jawbone and surrounding structures.

nodes.description: Numbness or altered sensation, particularly when the inferior alveolar nerve is involved; occurs more often in advanced disease.

evidence.reference_title: New Ameloblastoma Cell Lines Enable Preclinical Study of Targeted Therapies.
evidence.snippet: AB tumors are locally invasive and destructive to the jawbone and surrounding structures.

nodes.description: Displacement and mobility of teeth within the affected jaw region, which can lead to tooth loss and impaired mastication.

evidence.reference_title: Global incidence and profile of ameloblastoma: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
evidence.snippet: A slight male preference (53%) was found, and the mandible appeared to be the preferred site.
